titleStmt.title: Letter from Hanoria Shanahan to the Lord Lieutenant, Lord Wimborne, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hanoria Shanahan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
A handwritten petition from Nora (Hanoria) Shanahan for an extension to a lease to continue a licensed premises in Portmagee, Co. Kerry for a further number of months beyond April 1916.
This petition is a follow up to an initial request in January (NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-646) that was granted. NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-14211 and 1644 also refer.
Lord Wimborne (1873 –1939) was Lord Lieutenant, a largely ceremonial role, from 1915 until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers 1916, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-5526a

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Memorial. To His Excellency
Lord Wimborne
Lord Lieutenant
of Ireland. Your Excellency: I thank you from my
heart for the concession favour of carrying on a public
business till the 20th April, pending the refurbishment of my house. I pray your clemency an extension until August or
the middle of September next for the following reasons. 1. Owing to the winter being on, funds not available
and materials unnecessarily high the building
could not be proceeded with. 2 Tradesmen being scarce in this locality, could
not afford to bring them from the city, therefore was
compelled up to the present time to wait on local tradesmen. 3 Owing to my back premises or stores being destroyed
by fire I have nowhere to retain my little stock,
therefore crave permission to retain it in the occupied
house, and to proceed with my business therein until
my house which is to be commenced immediately,
is rebuilt. I ask the Almighty to grant you your favours,
to assist you in your need, and to grant you long
life and happiness in our midst is the humble prayer
of a helpless widow and her orphan children. Nora Shanahan
